description: Not dangerous or frightened of people; domesticated or gentle through familiarity. 'Woenu' names the quality of tameness—the trust built between species, wildness gentled through relationship.
sound_symbolism: The word settles near. 'Wo' begins with gentle approach, wariness fading. The vowel 'e' opens to trust, fear releasing. Then 'nu' rests in proximity, tameness achieved.
grammatical_notes: Functions as an adjective: 'woenu nimae' (tame animal). Opposite of 'waeshi' (wild). Implies relationship and trust, not merely control.

pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Tame presence calms—gentled beings create safety through their ease.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Tame animals were partners—domestication created mutual relationships, not mere dominion.
